title: Evidence Foundry Release Registry
description: Validates releases/registry.json (evidence-foundry-e1 P1-T5/OQ-4/FR-14, ADR-0005). The registry itself does not ship in this task -- P3-T4 creates the seed file (`{ "schemaVersion": 1, "entries": [] }`) and its `register` append-only writer; this task ships only the schema and its fixtures, per this task's own acceptance criteria. Document shape is deliberately `{ schemaVersion, entries[] }`, not a bare array, so the document itself can be schema-versioned independently of its entry shape. Each entry binds EXACTLY the OQ-4 field list -- `version`, `moduleId`, `packDigest`, `manifestDigest`, `signature`, `signedAt`, `supersedes`, `withdrawalState`, `withdrawnAt`, `withdrawalReason` -- nothing more: `additionalProperties: false` on `$defs/registryEntry` makes any surveillance/re-verify-cadence/materiality-class/signer-identity field (ADR-0007's unaccepted taxonomy, DF-E2-01/DF-E2-03 territory) a structural rejection, not a documentation omission. `signature`/`signedAt`/`supersedes`/`withdrawnAt`/`withdrawalReason` are `type: "null"` -- always exactly `null` under this schema version, because no E1 code path may ever populate them (a real signature only ever lives on the release-manifest's own signature slot, schemas/release-manifest.schema.json, gated per P1-T5's `dryRun`/`signature` mechanism and ultimately gate G2; a registry entry is an integrity record ABOUT a signed candidate, never the candidate's own signature bearer). `withdrawalState` is `const: "none"` -- E1 never sets any other value; DF-E2-03 extends this field family later, not this plan. Append-only-ness (no entry may ever be mutated or removed once written) is a cross-record, git-history-level invariant this single-document schema cannot express (no keyword here compares this document to its own prior committed state) -- `tools/release-sign`'s `register` verb (P3-T4) enforces it procedurally, the same two-layer approach P2-T3's review-record hash-chain validator uses. Structural validity here never implies clinical validity, safety, or that any human signing custodian (gate G2) or release authorizer (gate G4) has acted -- every entry this schema accepts has `signature: null`.

schemaVersion.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Integer registry-document-shape version, bumped only when this schema's own shape changes (mirrors schemas/review-record.schema.json's `schemaVersion` convention).

entries.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Append-only per FR-14: `[]` is legal (the P3-T4 seed file's own initial state, and the state of any module with no registered release yet); every element must independently satisfy `$defs/registryEntry`. This schema does not, and cannot, enforce ordering, uniqueness, or that no prior entry has been mutated/removed -- those are P3-T4's `register` writer's and its append-only validator's job, exercised against this document's git history, not this document's instantaneous shape.

registryEntry.description: Exactly the OQ-4 field list -- no field outside this list is legal (`additionalProperties: false`). Every REQUIRED key here must be present as a key, even when its value is legitimately/structurally always `null` under this schema version -- an omitted key is a schema violation, not a shorthand for `null` (matches schemas/module-manifest.schema.json's `approvedBy: []`-must-be-present convention for the same D-4-adjacent honesty reason: a missing key cannot be told apart from a validator bug, an explicit `null` can only be told apart from a deliberate design decision).

version.description: REQUIRED, non-null. The registered module release's own version string (matches the release-manifest's `packVersion`, once a real, non-`-proposal` release version exists -- E1 never registers one; see this schema's top-level description).

moduleId.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Same pattern as schemas/release-manifest.schema.json's `moduleId` and schemas/module-manifest.schema.json's module identity field -- must equal a real `MODULE_IDS` entry (src/modules/registry.js), not schema-enforced here (this schema validates the document in isolation).

packDigest.description: REQUIRED, non-null. SHA-256 of the canonicalized kb-pack contents this registry entry attests to -- same `sha256:<64 hex>` pattern schemas/release-manifest.schema.json's `testCorpusHash`/`traceabilityHash` already use, not invented ad hoc.

manifestDigest.description: REQUIRED, non-null. SHA-256 of the canonicalized release-manifest bytes (P5-T5's canonical serialization, the same bytes `tools/release-sign`'s `sign`/`verify` verbs operate over) this registry entry attests to.

signature.description: REQUIRED key, and under this schema version it MUST be exactly `null` -- E1 registers no signed release (no code path may populate this; a real signature, once G2 clears, lives on the release-manifest's own `signature` slot, never here). Mirrors schemas/module-manifest.schema.json's `approvedBy` `maxItems: 0` idiom for an object-typed field: forced-empty by `type`, not merely by convention. Raising this from `"null"` to admit a real value is the deliberate, reviewable, separately-gated act (post-G2) that this schema does not perform.

signedAt.description: REQUIRED key, always `null` in E1 -- no signature exists to timestamp yet (see `signature`).

supersedes.description: REQUIRED key, always `null` in E1 -- E1 registers at most a first entry per module; a prior-version supersession chain is E2/DF-E2-01 territory (surveillance/update/registry engine), not this plan.

withdrawalState.description: REQUIRED key, pinned to the single legal value `"none"` in E1 -- `const`, not `enum`, so a `"withdrawn"` (or any other) value is a hard schema violation, not merely a policy violation. DF-E2-03 (withdraw/rollback machinery) is the future, separately-gated extension point for this field; E1 ships no code path that can set anything else.

withdrawnAt.description: REQUIRED key, always `null` in E1 -- reinforces `withdrawalState: "none"` (a withdrawal timestamp cannot exist without a withdrawal).

withdrawalReason.description: REQUIRED key, always `null` in E1 -- reinforces `withdrawalState: "none"` (a withdrawal reason cannot exist without a withdrawal).
